items.title: JobHop v2: A Large-Scale Career Trajectory Dataset from Unstructured Resumes
items.summary: JobHop v2 is a career trajectory dataset containing 355,315 multilingual resumes annotated with ESCO occupational codes, temporal data, and education levels, extracted from raw resumes using LLMs.

items.title: MET: Theory-Grounded and Culture-Aware Multilingual Moral Reasoning
items.summary: MCLASH is a new multilingual moral decision-making benchmark, evaluated alongside MET, a two-step theory-grounded prompting method designed to capture culturally situated moral intuitions across languages.

items.title: VoxENES 2026: Benchmarking Generalization of Speech Spoofing Detectors Against LLM-Era TTS and Voice Conversion
items.summary: VoxENES 2026 is a bilingual benchmark of 53,628 audio samples for evaluating speech spoofing detectors against modern LLM-era text-to-speech and voice conversion systems under ten post-processing conditions.

items.title: Announcements Jun 30, 2026 Claude Science, an AI workbench for scientists, is now available Claude Science is a customizable app that integrates the tools and packages researchers most often use, produces auditable artifacts, and provides flexible access to computing resources.
items.summary: Anthropic released Claude Science on June 30, 2026, an AI workbench for scientists that integrates research tools, produces auditable artifacts, and provides flexible computing resource access.

items.title: Announcements Jun 30, 2026 Redeploying Fable 5 Fable 5 returns globally July 1. We're also proposing an industry-wide framework for scoring jailbreak severity, together with Amazon, Microsoft, Google, and other Glasswing partners.
items.summary: Anthropic redeployed Fable 5 globally on July 1, 2026, and proposed an industry-wide jailbreak severity scoring framework in partnership with Amazon, Microsoft, Google, and other Glasswing partners.

items.title: Empowering India’s next generation of innovators with ATL Saathi
items.summary: Google and AIM launched ATL Saathi, a Gemini-powered AI tool designed to assist Indian educators in robotics laboratories.

items.title: How data science teams use ChatGPT Work
items.summary: OpenAI published a guide demonstrating how data science teams can utilize ChatGPT Work to generate root-cause briefs, KPI memos, scoped analyses, and dashboard specifications.

items.title: How sales teams use ChatGPT Work
items.summary: OpenAI published a guide demonstrating how sales teams can utilize ChatGPT Work to generate pipeline briefs, meeting preparation packets, forecast reviews, and account plans.

items.title: Inviting hard questions Announcements Jul 9, 2026 We’re asking the public for their hardest questions about AI, and committing to show our work as we address them.
items.summary: Anthropic announced an initiative on July 9, 2026, inviting the public to submit difficult questions about AI, committing to share their work as they address them.

items.title: Modernizing the Meta Ads Service With an Open-Source Kernel Scheduler
items.summary: Meta deployed sched_ext, an upstream BPF-based extensible scheduling framework, to build a custom Linux kernel scheduling policy that resolved latency regressions across its ad serving fleet.

items.title: Get your ESP32 Sunny Side Up with this Solar Dev Board
items.summary: A new solar-powered development board has been designed specifically for the versatile ESP32 microcontroller, targeting off-grid and low-power hardware projects.

items.title: Security updates for Monday
items.summary: Security updates have been issued for multiple Linux distributions, including Debian, Fedora, Mageia, Oracle, Slackware, and SUSE, covering packages such as chromium, openssh, and the kernel.

items.title: Security updates for Tuesday
items.summary: Security updates have been released for AlmaLinux, Debian, Fedora, Mageia, SUSE, and Ubuntu, covering critical packages including the Linux kernel, systemd, golang, qemu, and openssh.

items.title: [$] Sending packets directly from BPF
items.summary: Researchers have developed a method to send Tetragon security monitoring data directly from BPF, eliminating the vulnerable user-space agent to prevent attackers from silencing alerts.

items.title: [$] Shielding running kernels against exploits with BPF
items.summary: Cisco is using BPF to prevent kernel exploit execution on custom devices, reducing vulnerability response times, though full effectiveness requires additional kernel hooks.

items.title: Final normal Debian bookworm release
items.summary: Debian has released the final normal update for Debian 12 ("bookworm"), transitioning the release to long-term support until 2028.

items.title: Upcoming: Codeforces Round (Div. 2)
items.summary: Codeforces Round (Div. 2) is scheduled for Saturday, July 18 at 14:35 UTC, with a duration of 2.0 hours.

items.title: Show HN: Jacquard, a programming language for AI-written, human-reviewed code
items.summary: Jacquard is a new programming language designed by AI for AI-written, human-reviewed code, featuring explicit permission requirements for filesystem access and visible side effects in function signatures.

items.title: Complexity Theory of Randomised Testing
items.summary: A new paper establishes complexity-theoretic foundations for randomized software testing, modeling generators as Turing transducers and proving that theoretically generable languages coincide with recursively enumerable languages.

items.title: On Thread Convergence
items.summary: A new control-flow graph convergence analysis helps compilers determine if barriers are guaranteed to synchronize all threads in a block, avoiding unnecessary code transformations on warp-synchronous hardware.

items.title: A Separation Logic for Parallel Time Complexity with Work and Span Credits
items.summary: Parcas is a concurrent separation logic for verifying the parallel time complexity of fork-join programs, utilizing work and span credits to bound execution costs.

items.title: AutoSLO: Practical Latency SLOs on Cloud Data Warehouses -- Extended Version
items.summary: AutoSLO is a latency-SLO-aware workload management framework for multi-cluster cloud data warehouses that dynamically scales compute clusters to prevent over-provisioning and SLO violations.

items.title: Mizzle: A Complete Concurrent Incorrectness Logic for Preventing False Alarms in Agentic Bug Finding
items.summary: Mizzle is a mechanized, complete concurrent incorrectness separation logic for OCaml, designed to eliminate LLM-generated false alarms in bug finding by requiring machine-checked proofs of reachability.

items.title: LizardByte/Sunshine
items.summary: Sunshine is a self-hosted, open-source game stream host for Moonlight, written in C++.

items.title: jackwener/wx-cli
items.summary: WeChat local data CLI with daemon architecture

items.title: Graphify-Labs/graphify
items.summary: AI coding assistant skill (Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, Cursor, Gemini CLI, and more). Turn any folder of code, SQL schemas, R scripts, shell scripts, docs, papers, images, or videos into a queryable knowledge graph. Ap…

items.title: PrefectHQ/prefect
items.summary: Prefect is a workflow orchestration framework for building resilient data pipelines in Python.

items.title: PrimeIntellect-ai/verifiers
items.summary: Our library for RL environments + evals

items.title: UZ-SLAMLab/ORB_SLAM3
items.summary: ORB-SLAM3: An Accurate Open-Source Library for Visual, Visual-Inertial and Multi-Map SLAM
